#!/usr/bin/env python3
import unittest

import torch

import substrate
import substrate.language as S
from substrate.testing import has_rocm


@substrate.jit
def gemm_mfma_16x16x16(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.u32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.u32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.f32),
):
    lane = S.thread_id(0)

    c_lane = S.full((4,), 0.0, S.f32)

    m_a = S.view(A[lane], S.Tensor((2, 4, 1), S.f16))
    m_b = S.view(B[lane], S.Tensor((2, 4, 1), S.f16))

    c_lane = S.amdgpu.mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32(m_a[0], m_b[0], c_lane)
    c_lane = S.amdgpu.mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32(m_a[1], m_b[1], c_lane)

    C[lane] = c_lane


@substrate.jit
def gemm_mfma_32x32x8_bf16(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 16), S.f32),
):
    lane = S.thread_id(0)

    c_lane = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    m_a = S.view(A[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))
    m_b = S.view(B[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))

    c_lane = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(m_a[0], m_b[0], c_lane)

    C[lane] = c_lane


@unittest.skipUnless(
    has_rocm(),
    "Requires ROCm/HIP with an AMD GPU.",
)
class TestGEMMSimple(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        """Set up test fixtures before each test method."""
        torch.manual_seed(0)
        self.M = 16
        self.N = 16
        self.K = 32
        self.WARP_SIZE = 64
        self.rtol = 1e-2
        self.atol = 1e-3

    def test_gemm_mfma_16x16x16(self):
        """Test simple GEMM with single MFMA operation (no tiling)."""
        if not torch.cuda.is_available():
            self.skipTest("CUDA is not available.")
        if not hasattr(torch.version, "hip") or torch.version.hip is None:
            self.skipTest("HIP is not available.")

        A = torch.randn((self.M, self.K), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")
        B = torch.randn((self.K, self.N), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")
        C = torch.zeros((self.M, self.N), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")

        expected = (A @ B).to(dtype=torch.float16, device="cpu")

        frag_size = self.K // 4  # mfma_16x16x16_f16_f32 loads 4 f16 elements

        A_shuffled = torch.zeros((self.WARP_SIZE, frag_size), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")
        for lane in range(self.WARP_SIZE):
            i = lane % 16
            k_block = lane // 16
            for t in range(frag_size):
                k = k_block * frag_size + t
                A_shuffled[lane, t] = A[i, k]

        B_shuffled = torch.zeros((self.WARP_SIZE, frag_size), dtype=torch.float16, device="cuda")
        for lane in range(self.WARP_SIZE):
            k_block = lane // 16
            j = lane % 16
            for t in range(frag_size):
                k = k_block * frag_size + t
                B_shuffled[lane, t] = B[k, j]

        A_packed = A_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(self.WARP_SIZE, frag_size // 2)
        B_packed = B_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(self.WARP_SIZE, frag_size // 2)
        C_shuffled = torch.zeros((self.WARP_SIZE, 4), dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")

        gemm_mfma_16x16x16[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (self.WARP_SIZE, 1, 1))](A_packed, B_packed, C_shuffled)

        for lane in range(self.WARP_SIZE):
            g = lane // 16
            j = lane % 16
            for t in range(4):
                C[4 * g + t, j] = C_shuffled[lane, t].to(torch.float16)

        actual = C.to("cpu")

        self.assertTrue(
            torch.allclose(actual, expected, rtol=self.rtol, atol=self.atol),
            msg=f"GEMM results do not match.\nExpected:\n{expected}\nActual:\n{actual}\n"
            f"Max absolute difference: {torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected))}",
        )

    def test_gemm_mfma_32x32x8_bf16(self):
        """Test GEMM with bf16 MFMA 32x32x8."""
        if not torch.cuda.is_available():
            self.skipTest("CUDA is not available.")
        if not hasattr(torch.version, "hip") or torch.version.hip is None:
            self.skipTest("HIP is not available.")

        m = 32
        n = 32
        k = 8
        warp_size = 64
        row_block = m // (warp_size // n)

        A = torch.randn((m, k), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda")
        B = torch.randn((k, n), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda")
        C = torch.zeros((m, n), dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")

        expected = (A @ B).to(dtype=torch.float32, device="cpu")

        frag_size = k // (warp_size // m)

        def permute_row(row: int) -> int:
            high = (row >> 2) & 0x7
            rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
            return (row & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

        A_shuffled = torch.zeros((warp_size, frag_size), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda")
        for lane in range(warp_size):
            i = permute_row(lane % m)
            k_block = lane // m
            for t in range(frag_size):
                kk = k_block * frag_size + t
                A_shuffled[lane, t] = A[i, kk]

        B_shuffled = torch.zeros((warp_size, frag_size), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device="cuda")
        for lane in range(warp_size):
            k_block = lane // n
            j = lane % n
            for t in range(frag_size):
                kk = k_block * frag_size + t
                B_shuffled[lane, t] = B[kk, j]

        A_packed = A_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(warp_size, frag_size // 2)
        B_packed = B_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(warp_size, frag_size // 2)
        C_shuffled = torch.zeros((warp_size, 16), dtype=torch.float32, device="cuda")

        gemm_mfma_32x32x8_bf16[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (warp_size, 1, 1))](
            A_packed, B_packed, C_shuffled
        )

        for lane in range(warp_size):
            g = lane // n
            j = lane % n
            for t in range(row_block):
                C[row_block * g + t, j] = C_shuffled[lane, t]

        actual = C.to("cpu")

        self.assertTrue(
            torch.allclose(actual, expected, rtol=self.rtol, atol=self.atol),
            msg=f"GEMM results do not match.\nExpected:\n{expected}\nActual:\n{actual}\n"
            f"Max absolute difference: {torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected))}",
        )


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main(verbosity=2)

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue! Looking at the test_gemm_mfma.py code:

1. For A: `i = permute_row(lane % m)` - this applies permutation
2. For B: `j = lane % n` - no permutation
3. For output: `C[row_block * g + t, j] = C_shuffled[lane, t]`

The key insight is that:
- `row_block = m // (warp_size // m)` = 32 // 2 = 16
- For output unpacking: `C[16 * g + t, j]` where `g = lane // n` and `j = lane % n`

Wait, this is different from the formula I'm using. Let me trace through:

For mfma_32x32x8_bf16:
- m = 32, n = 32, k = 8
- warp_size = 64
- row_block = 32 // (64 // 32) = 32 // 2 = 16

For output unpacking:
- lane 0: g = 0, j = 0, t = 0..15 -> C[0..15, 0]
- lane 1: g = 0, j = 1, t = 0..15 -> C[0..15, 1]
- ...
- lane 31: g = 0, j = 31, t = 0..15 -> C[0..15, 31]
- lane 32: g = 1, j = 0, t = 0..15 -> C[16..31, 0]
- lane 33: g = 1, j = 1, t = 0..15 -> C[16..31, 1]
- ...

So the output layout is:
- Lanes 0-31 hold rows 0-15, columns 0-31
- Lanes 32-63 hold rows 16-31, columns 0-31

And each lane holds 16 elements (the `t` dimension).

But wait, this is WITHOUT permutation in the output unpacking! The permutation is only applied to the INPUT (A matrix).

So the formula for output row should be:
- row = 16 * (lane // 32) + t = 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx

This is simpler than what I had! Let me check:

In my kernel, I had:
```python
row = 8 * (acc_idx // 4) + 4 * (lane // 32) + (acc_idx % 4)
```

But the working test uses:
```python
row = row_block * g + t = 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx
```

These are different! Let me compute both:

My formula:
- acc_idx=0, lane=0: row = 0 + 0 + 0 = 0
- acc_idx=1, lane=0: row = 0 + 0 + 1 = 1
- acc_idx=0, lane=32: row = 0 + 4 + 0 = 4
- acc_idx=15, lane=0: row = 8*3 + 0 + 3 = 27
- acc_idx=15, lane=32: row = 8*3 + 4 + 3 = 31

Working formula:
- acc_idx=0, lane=0: row = 0 + 0 = 0
- acc_idx=1, lane=0: row = 0 + 1 = 1
- acc_idx=0, lane=32: row = 16 + 0 = 16
- acc_idx=15, lane=0: row = 0 + 15 = 15
- acc_idx=15, lane=32: row = 16 + 15 = 31

These are completely different! My formula gives a swizzled layout, while the working formula is straightforward.

Let me update the kernel to use the correct formula.

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import substrate
import substrate.language as S


# Input/output shapes
N = 16
IN_CHANNELS = 16
IN_H = 1024
IN_W = 1024
OUT_CHANNELS = 128
KERNEL_H = 3
KERNEL_W = 3
OUT_H = 1022
OUT_W = 1022
KERNEL_AREA = KERNEL_H * KERNEL_W

# MFMA tiling parameters
BLOCK_M = 128
BLOCK_N = 128
WARP_TILE_M = 64
WARP_TILE_N = 64
MFMA_TILE_M = 32
MFMA_TILE_N = 32
MFMA_TILE_K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64
NUM_WARPS = 4
THREADS_PER_BLOCK = WARP_SIZE * NUM_WARPS

# Derived constants
HW_OUT = OUT_H * OUT_W
K_FLAT = IN_CHANNELS * KERNEL_AREA
M_FLAT = N * HW_OUT

INPUT0_SHAPE = (N, IN_CHANNELS, IN_H, IN_W)
OUTPUT_SHAPE = (N, OUT_CHANNELS, OUT_H, OUT_W)
WEIGHT_SHAPE = (OUT_CHANNELS, IN_CHANNELS, KERNEL_H, KERNEL_W)


def _ceil_div(x, y):
    return (x + y - 1) // y


def _launch():
    return (
        (_ceil_div(M_FLAT, BLOCK_M), _ceil_div(OUT_CHANNELS, BLOCK_N), 1),
        (THREADS_PER_BLOCK, 1, 1),
    )


@substrate.jit
def fused_kernel(
    X: S.Tensor((16, 16, 1024, 1024), S.f32),
    W: S.Tensor((128, 16, 3, 3), S.f32),
    Y: S.Tensor((16, 128, 1022, 1022), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    warp_id = tid // 64
    lane = tid % 64

    # 2x2 warp grid
    warp_row = warp_id // 2
    warp_col = warp_id % 2

    # Lane layout for MFMA
    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # Block tile base
    group_m_base = S.block_id(0) * BLOCK_M
    group_n_base = S.block_id(1) * BLOCK_N

    # Accumulators for the 2x2 MFMA tiles per warp
    acc00 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc01 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc10 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc11 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # K tiles: K_FLAT = 144, MFMA_TILE_K = 8, so 18 tiles
    for k_tile in S.range(18):
        # Fragments for this K tile - use make_local for proper register allocation
        a_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        b_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        b_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

        # Initialize fragments to 0
        for e in S.range(4):
            a_frag0[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            a_frag1[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            b_frag0[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            b_frag1[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)

        # For A: lane l loads from logical row lane_col
        m0 = group_m_base + warp_row * 64 + lane_col
        m1 = m0 + 32

        # For B: no permutation needed
        n0 = group_n_base + warp_col * 64 + lane_col
        n1 = n0 + 32

        # Load 4 elements for the K dimension
        for e in S.range(4):
            k_idx = k_tile * 8 + lane_k_base + e

            # Decode k_idx into (ic, kh, kw)
            ic = k_idx // KERNEL_AREA
            spatial = k_idx % KERNEL_AREA
            kh = spatial // KERNEL_W
            kw = spatial % KERNEL_W

            # Load A fragments (input activations)
            if m0 < M_FLAT:
                batch0 = m0 // HW_OUT
                hw0 = m0 % HW_OUT
                oh0 = hw0 // OUT_W
                ow0 = hw0 % OUT_W
                ih0 = oh0 + kh
                iw0 = ow0 + kw
                a_frag0[e] = S.convert(X[batch0, ic, ih0, iw0], S.bf16)

            if m1 < M_FLAT:
                batch1 = m1 // HW_OUT
                hw1 = m1 % HW_OUT
                oh1 = hw1 // OUT_W
                ow1 = hw1 % OUT_W
                ih1 = oh1 + kh
                iw1 = ow1 + kw
                a_frag1[e] = S.convert(X[batch1, ic, ih1, iw1], S.bf16)

            # Load B fragments (weights)
            if n0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
                b_frag0[e] = S.convert(W[n0, ic, kh, kw], S.bf16)

            if n1 < OUT_CHANNELS:
                b_frag1[e] = S.convert(W[n1, ic, kh, kw], S.bf16)

        # MFMA operations for the 2x2 subtile array
        acc00 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag0, acc00)
        acc01 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag1, acc01)
        acc10 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag0, acc10)
        acc11 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag1, acc11)

    # Writeback: use original formula (no inverse permutation)
    # The MFMA output layout is swizzled, matching the permuted input
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row_base0 = group_m_base + warp_row * 64
        row_base1 = row_base0 + 32
        col0 = group_n_base + warp_col * 64 + lane_col
        col1 = col0 + 32

        row00 = row_base0 + 8 * (acc_idx // 4) + 4 * (lane // 32) + (acc_idx % 4)
        row10 = row_base1 + 8 * (acc_idx // 4) + 4 * (lane // 32) + (acc_idx % 4)

        # tm = 0, tn = 0
        if row00 < M_FLAT and col0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            batch = row00 // HW_OUT
            hw = row00 % HW_OUT
            oh = hw // OUT_W
            ow = hw % OUT_W
            Y[batch, col0, oh, ow] = acc00[acc_idx]

        # tm = 0, tn = 1
        if row00 < M_FLAT and col1 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            batch = row00 // HW_OUT
            hw = row00 % HW_OUT
            oh = hw // OUT_W
            ow = hw % OUT_W
            Y[batch, col1, oh, ow] = acc01[acc_idx]

        # tm = 1, tn = 0
        if row10 < M_FLAT and col0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            batch = row10 // HW_OUT
            hw = row10 % HW_OUT
            oh = hw // OUT_W
            ow = hw % OUT_W
            Y[batch, col0, oh, ow] = acc10[acc_idx]

        # tm = 1, tn = 1
        if row10 < M_FLAT and col1 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            batch = row10 // HW_OUT
            hw = row10 % HW_OUT
            oh = hw // OUT_W
            ow = hw % OUT_W
            Y[batch, col1, oh, ow] = acc11[acc_idx]


class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    def __init__(
        self,
        in_channels,
        out_channels,
        kernel_size,
        stride=1,
        padding=0,
        dilation=1,
        groups=1,
        bias=False,
    ):
        super(ModelNew, self).__init__()
        self.conv2d = nn.Conv2d(
            in_channels,
            out_channels,
            kernel_size,
            stride=stride,
            padding=padding,
            dilation=dilation,
            groups=groups,
            bias=bias,
        )
        self._cached_weight = None
        self._cached_weight_key = None
        self._cached_output = None
        self._cached_buffer_key = None

    def _check_supported(self):
        if self.conv2d.in_channels != IN_CHANNELS:
            raise RuntimeError(f"This fused kernel only supports in_channels={IN_CHANNELS}.")
        if self.conv2d.out_channels != OUT_CHANNELS:
            raise RuntimeError(f"This fused kernel only supports out_channels={OUT_CHANNELS}.")
        if tuple(self.conv2d.kernel_size) != (KERNEL_H, KERNEL_W):
            raise RuntimeError(f"This fused kernel only supports kernel_size={(KERNEL_H, KERNEL_W)}.")
        if tuple(self.conv2d.stride) != (1, 1):
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel only supports stride=1.")
        if tuple(self.conv2d.padding) != (0, 0):
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel only supports padding=0.")
        if tuple(self.conv2d.dilation) != (1, 1):
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel only supports dilation=1.")
        if self.conv2d.groups != 1:
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel only supports groups=1.")
        if self.conv2d.bias is not None:
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel does not support bias.")

    def _get_cached_weight(self, x):
        self._check_supported()
        weight = self.conv2d.weight
        key = (
            x.device.type,
            x.device.index,
            x.dtype,
            weight.data_ptr(),
        )
        if self._cached_weight_key != key:
            self._cached_weight = weight.detach().to(device=x.device, dtype=x.dtype).contiguous()
            self._cached_weight_key = key
        return self._cached_weight

    def _get_cached_output(self, x):
        key = (x.device.type, x.device.index)
        if self._cached_buffer_key != key:
            self._cached_output = torch.empty(OUTPUT_SHAPE, device=x.device, dtype=torch.float32)
            self._cached_buffer_key = key
        return self._cached_output

    def forward(self, x):
        self._check_supported()
        if tuple(x.shape) != INPUT0_SHAPE or x.dtype != torch.float32:
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel only supports the benchmark input shape and dtype.")
        if not x.is_cuda:
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel requires a CUDA/HIP device tensor.")
        if not x.is_contiguous():
            raise RuntimeError("This fused kernel requires contiguous input for the graph-safe path.")

        w = self._get_cached_weight(x)
        y = self._get_cached_output(x)
        fused_kernel[_launch](x, w, y, num_warps=NUM_WARPS)
        return y

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Simplified Conv2D: 1x16x4x4 input, 4 output channels, 3x3 kernel
# Output: 1x4x2x2

N = 1
IN_CHANNELS = 16
IN_H = 4
IN_W = 4
OUT_CHANNELS = 4
KERNEL_H = 3
KERNEL_W = 3
OUT_H = 2
OUT_W = 2
KERNEL_AREA = KERNEL_H * KERNEL_W

# MFMA tiling
BLOCK_M = 4  # M_FLAT = 1 * 2 * 2 = 4
BLOCK_N = 4
WARP_SIZE = 64
NUM_WARPS = 1
THREADS_PER_BLOCK = WARP_SIZE * NUM_WARPS

HW_OUT = OUT_H * OUT_W
K_FLAT = IN_CHANNELS * KERNEL_AREA
M_FLAT = N * HW_OUT

@substrate.jit
def simple_conv_kernel(
    X: S.Tensor((1, 16, 4, 4), S.f32),
    W: S.Tensor((4, 16, 3, 3), S.f32),
    Y: S.Tensor((1, 4, 2, 2), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    group_m_base = S.block_id(0) * BLOCK_M
    group_n_base = S.block_id(1) * BLOCK_N

    acc = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # K tiles: K_FLAT = 144, MFMA_TILE_K = 8, so 18 tiles
    for k_tile in S.range(18):
        a_frag = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        b_frag = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

        for e in S.range(4):
            a_frag[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            b_frag[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)

        m0 = group_m_base + lane_col
        n0 = group_n_base + lane_col

        for e in S.range(4):
            k_idx = k_tile * 8 + lane_k_base + e

            ic = k_idx // KERNEL_AREA
            spatial = k_idx % KERNEL_AREA
            kh = spatial // KERNEL_W
            kw = spatial % KERNEL_W

            if m0 < M_FLAT:
                batch0 = m0 // HW_OUT
                hw0 = m0 % HW_OUT
                oh0 = hw0 // OUT_W
                ow0 = hw0 % OUT_W
                ih0 = oh0 + kh
                iw0 = ow0 + kw
                a_frag[e] = S.convert(X[batch0, ic, ih0, iw0], S.bf16)

            if n0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
                b_frag[e] = S.convert(W[n0, ic, kh, kw], S.bf16)

        acc = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag, b_frag, acc)

    # Writeback
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row = group_m_base + 8 * (acc_idx // 4) + 4 * (lane // 32) + (acc_idx % 4)
        col = group_n_base + lane_col

        if row < M_FLAT and col < OUT_CHANNELS:
            batch = row // HW_OUT
            hw = row % HW_OUT
            oh = hw // OUT_W
            ow = hw % OUT_W
            Y[batch, col, oh, ow] = acc[acc_idx]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import torch.nn.functional as F

    x = torch.randn((1, 16, 4, 4), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    w = torch.randn((4, 16, 3, 3), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    y = torch.zeros((1, 4, 2, 2), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    expected = F.conv2d(x, w, stride=1, padding=0)

    simple_conv_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (64, 1, 1))](x, w, y)

    actual = y.cpu()
    expected_cpu = expected.cpu()

    max_diff = torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected_cpu)).item()
    print(f'Max diff: {max_diff}')
    print(f'Pass: {torch.allclose(actual, expected_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')

    print(f'actual: {actual}')
    print(f'expected: {expected_cpu}')

import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test Conv2D with exact MFMA pattern from working test
# 32x32x8 MFMA, single tile

N = 1
IN_CHANNELS = 16
IN_H = 35
IN_W = 35
OUT_CHANNELS = 32
KERNEL_H = 3
KERNEL_W = 3
OUT_H = 33
OUT_W = 33
KERNEL_AREA = KERNEL_H * KERNEL_W

BLOCK_M = 32
BLOCK_N = 32
WARP_SIZE = 64
NUM_WARPS = 1

HW_OUT = OUT_H * OUT_W
K_FLAT = IN_CHANNELS * KERNEL_AREA
M_FLAT = N * HW_OUT

def permute_row(row: int) -> int:
    high = (row >> 2) & 0x7
    rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
    return (row & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

@substrate.jit
def conv_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),  # Pre-packed A
    B: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),  # Pre-packed B
    C: S.Tensor((64, 16), S.f32),  # Output
):
    lane = S.thread_id(0)

    acc = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # View packed data as bf16
    m_a = S.view(A[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))
    m_b = S.view(B[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))

    acc = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(m_a[0], m_b[0], acc)

    C[lane] = acc


def test_conv_with_mfma():
    # Create input and weight
    x = torch.randn((N, IN_CHANNELS, IN_H, IN_W), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    w = torch.randn((OUT_CHANNELS, IN_CHANNELS, KERNEL_H, KERNEL_W), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    # Compute expected using PyTorch
    expected_full = F.conv2d(x, w, stride=1, padding=0)

    # For testing, just do a single 32x32 tile
    # Take the first 32 output positions and first 32 output channels
    expected = expected_full[0, :32, :32, 0].T  # Shape (32, 32)

    # Flatten input activations for the GEMM
    # For output position (oh, ow), we need input at (oh:oh+3, ow:ow+3)
    # Let's test with output position (0, 0) - (0, 31)
    # Actually, let's just test a simple GEMM

    # Create simple GEMM test
    m, n, k = 32, 32, 8
    A = torch.randn((m, k), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')

    C = torch.zeros((m, n), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    expected = (A @ B).to(dtype=torch.float32, device='cpu')

    frag_size = k // (WARP_SIZE // m)  # 8 // 2 = 4

    # Pre-shuffle A
    A_shuffled = torch.zeros((WARP_SIZE, frag_size), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    for lane in range(WARP_SIZE):
        i = permute_row(lane % m)
        k_block = lane // m
        for t in range(frag_size):
            kk = k_block * frag_size + t
            A_shuffled[lane, t] = A[i, kk]

    # Pre-shuffle B
    B_shuffled = torch.zeros((WARP_SIZE, frag_size), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    for lane in range(WARP_SIZE):
        k_block = lane // n
        j = lane % n
        for t in range(frag_size):
            kk = k_block * frag_size + t
            B_shuffled[lane, t] = B[kk, j]

    # Pack to u32

    actual = C.to('cpu')

    max_diff = torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected)).item()
    print(f'Max diff: {max_diff}')

import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test Conv2D with exact MFMA pattern from working test
# 32x32x8 MFMA, single tile

N = 1
IN_CHANNELS = 16
IN_H = 35
IN_W = 35
OUT_CHANNELS = 32
KERNEL_H = 3
KERNEL_W = 3
OUT_H = 33
OUT_W = 33
KERNEL_AREA = KERNEL_H * KERNEL_W

BLOCK_M = 32
BLOCK_N = 32
WARP_SIZE = 64
NUM_WARPS = 1

HW_OUT = OUT_H * OUT_W
K_FLAT = IN_CHANNELS * KERNEL_AREA
M_FLAT = N * HW_OUT

def permute_row(row: int) -> int:
    high = (row >> 2) & 0x7
    rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
    return (row & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

@substrate.jit
def conv_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),  # Pre-packed A
    B: S.Tensor((64, 2), S.u32),  # Pre-packed B
    C: S.Tensor((64, 16), S.f32),  # Output
):
    lane = S.thread_id(0)

    acc = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # View packed data as bf16
    m_a = S.view(A[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))
    m_b = S.view(B[lane], S.Tensor((1, 4, 1), S.bf16))

    acc = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(m_a[0], m_b[0], acc)

    C[lane] = acc


def test_conv_with_mfma():
    # Create input and weight
    x = torch.randn((N, IN_CHANNELS, IN_H, IN_W), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    w = torch.randn((OUT_CHANNELS, IN_CHANNELS, KERNEL_H, KERNEL_W), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    # Compute expected using PyTorch
    expected_full = F.conv2d(x, w, stride=1, padding=0)

    # For testing, just do a single 32x32 tile
    # Take the first 32 output positions and first 32 output channels
    expected = expected_full[0, :32, :32, 0].T  # Shape (32, 32)

    # Flatten input activations for the GEMM
    # For output position (oh, ow), we need input at (oh:oh+3, ow:ow+3)
    # Let's test with output position (0, 0) - (0, 31)
    # Actually, let's just test a simple GEMM

    # Create simple GEMM test
    m, n, k = 32, 32, 8
    A = torch.randn((m, k), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    B = torch.randn((k, n), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    C = torch.zeros((m, n), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    expected = (A @ B).to(dtype=torch.float32, device='cpu')

    frag_size = k // (WARP_SIZE // m)  # 8 // 2 = 4

    # Pre-shuffle A
    A_shuffled = torch.zeros((WARP_SIZE, frag_size), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    for lane in range(WARP_SIZE):
        i = permute_row(lane % m)
        k_block = lane // m
        for t in range(frag_size):
            kk = k_block * frag_size + t
            A_shuffled[lane, t] = A[i, kk]

    # Pre-shuffle B
    B_shuffled = torch.zeros((WARP_SIZE, frag_size), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    for lane in range(WARP_SIZE):
        k_block = lane // n
        j = lane % n
        for t in range(frag_size):
            kk = k_block * frag_size + t
            B_shuffled[lane, t] = B[kk, j]

    # Pack to u32
    A_packed = A_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(WARP_SIZE, frag_size // 2)
    B_packed = B_shuffled.view(torch.int32).view(WARP_SIZE, frag_size // 2)
    C_shuffled = torch.zeros((WARP_SIZE, 16), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    conv_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (WARP_SIZE, 1, 1))](A_packed, B_packed, C_shuffled)

    # Unpack output
    row_block = m // (WARP_SIZE // m)  # 16
    for lane in range(WARP_SIZE):
        g = lane // n
        j = lane % n
        for t in range(row_block):
            C[row_block * g + t, j] = C_shuffled[lane, t]

    actual = C.to('cpu')

    max_diff = torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected)).item()
    print(f'Max diff: {max_diff}')
    print(f'Pass: {torch.allclose(actual, expected, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')

    if max_diff > 0.1:
        print(f'actual[0]: {actual[0]}')
        print(f'expected[0]: {expected[0]}')


if __name__ == "__main__":
    test_conv_with_mfma()

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test with 2x2 MFMA tiles (like the full kernel)
# 64x64 output per warp, split into 2x2 array of 32x32 MFMA tiles

M_FLAT = 64
OUT_CHANNELS = 64
K_FLAT = 16
KERNEL_AREA = 4  # 2x2 kernel for simplicity

WARP_SIZE = 64
NUM_WARPS = 1

@substrate.jit
def gemm_2x2_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 16), S.f32),  # Input A
    B: S.Tensor((64, 16), S.f32),  # Weights B (transposed for simplicity)
    C: S.Tensor((64, 64), S.f32),  # Output C
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # Accumulators for 2x2 MFMA tiles
    acc00 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc01 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc10 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc11 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # K tiles: K_FLAT = 16, MFMA_TILE_K = 8, so 2 tiles
    for k_tile in S.range(2):
        a_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        b_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        b_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

        for e in S.range(4):
            a_frag0[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            a_frag1[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            b_frag0[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            b_frag1[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)

        # For A: first 32 rows and next 32 rows
        m0 = lane_col  # Row 0-31
        m1 = lane_col + 32  # Row 32-63

        # For B: first 32 columns and next 32 columns
        n0 = lane_col  # Column 0-31
        n1 = lane_col + 32  # Column 32-63

        for e in S.range(4):
            k_idx = k_tile * 8 + lane_k_base + e

            # Load A fragments
            if m0 < M_FLAT:
                a_frag0[e] = S.convert(A[m0, k_idx], S.bf16)
            if m1 < M_FLAT:
                a_frag1[e] = S.convert(A[m1, k_idx], S.bf16)

            # Load B fragments
            if n0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
                b_frag0[e] = S.convert(B[n0, k_idx], S.bf16)
            if n1 < OUT_CHANNELS:
                b_frag1[e] = S.convert(B[n1, k_idx], S.bf16)

        # MFMA operations
        acc00 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag0, acc00)
        acc01 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag1, acc01)
        acc10 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag0, acc10)
        acc11 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag1, acc11)

    # Writeback
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row0 = 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx  # Row within first 32 rows
        row1 = 32 + 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx  # Row within second 32 rows
        col0 = lane_col  # Column within first 32 columns
        col1 = lane_col + 32  # Column within second 32 columns

        if row0 < M_FLAT and col0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            C[row0, col0] = acc00[acc_idx]
        if row0 < M_FLAT and col1 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            C[row0, col1] = acc01[acc_idx]
        if row1 < M_FLAT and col0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            C[row1, col0] = acc10[acc_idx]
        if row1 < M_FLAT and col1 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            C[row1, col1] = acc11[acc_idx]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    A = torch.randn((64, 16), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    B = torch.randn((64, 16), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    C = torch.zeros((64, 64), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    # Expected: C = A @ B.T
    expected = A @ B.T

    gemm_2x2_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (64, 1, 1))](A, B, C)

    actual = C.cpu()
    expected_cpu = expected.cpu()

    max_diff = torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected_cpu)).item()
    print(f'Max diff: {max_diff}')
    print(f'Pass: {torch.allclose(actual, expected_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')

    if max_diff > 0.1:
        print(f'\nFirst row comparison:')
        print(f'actual[0]: {actual[0, :8]}')
        print(f'expected[0]: {expected_cpu[0, :8]}')
        print(f'\nRow 32 comparison:')
        print(f'actual[32]: {actual[32, :8]}')
        print(f'expected[32]: {expected_cpu[32, :8]}')

The 2x2 tile test fails. Notice that row 0 and row 32 have the same values in the actual output - this is a bug!

The issue is that both lane 0 and lane 32 are loading from `m0 = lane_col` (which is 0 for both) and `m1 = lane_col + 32` (which is 32 for both). So they're loading the same data and producing the same output.

But wait, the MFMA is a collective operation. All lanes in a warp participate. Each lane should produce different output rows based on its lane ID.

Let me think about this more carefully. For a single MFMA 32x32x8:
- All 64 lanes participate
- Each lane contributes 4 bf16 values for A and 4 bf16 for B
- Each lane produces 16 f32 accumulator values

The output rows are distributed among lanes:
- Lanes 0-31: rows 0-15
- Lanes 32-63: rows 16-31

So for a 32x32 output, the output row formula is `16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx`.

Now, for a 2x2 array of MFMA tiles:
- We need to compute a 64x64 output
- Tile (0,0): rows 0-31, columns 0-31
- Tile (0,1): rows 0-31, columns 32-63
- Tile (1,0): rows 32-63, columns 0-31
- Tile (1,1): rows 32-63, columns 32-63

Each MFMA instruction computes a 32x32 tile. The a_frag and b_frag determine which rows/columns are computed.

- lane 0: k_block = 0, i = permute_row(0) = 0, loads A[0, 0:4]
- lane 1: k_block = 0, i = permute_row(1) = 1, loads A[1, 0:4]
- ...
- lane 31: k_block = 0, i = permute_row(31), loads A[permute_row(31), 0:4]
- lane 32: k_block = 1, i = permute_row(0) = 0, loads A[0, 4:8]
- lane 33: k_block = 1, i = permute_row(1) = 1, loads A[1, 4:8]
- ...

So lanes 0-31 load K values 0-3 for all 32 rows, and lanes 32-63 load K values 4-7 for all 32 rows.

For B:
- lane 0: k_block = 0, j = 0, loads B[0:4, 0]
- lane 1: k_block = 0, j = 1, loads B[0:4, 1]
- ...
- lane 31: k_block = 0, j = 31, loads B[0:4, 31]
- lane 32: k_block = 1, j = 0, loads B[4:8, 0]
- ...

So lanes 0-31 load K values 0-3 for all 32 columns, and lanes 32-63 load K values 4-7 for all 32 columns.

For output:
- lane 0: g = 0, j = 0, writes C[0:16, 0]
- lane 1: g = 0, j = 1, writes C[0:16, 1]
- ...
- lane 31: g = 0, j = 31, writes C[0:16, 31]
- lane 32: g = 1, j = 0, writes C[16:32, 0]
- lane 33: g = 1, j = 1, writes C[16:32, 1]
- ...

So:
- Lanes 0-31 output rows 0-15
- Lanes 32-63 output rows 16-31

Now, the key insight is:
- Lane l holds A data for row permute_row(l % 32)
- Lane l outputs for rows 16*(l//32) + t

So the input row for lane l is permute_row(l % 32), but the output row is 16*(l//32) + t.

This means:

- Lane 32: input row 0, output rows 16-31

So lanes 0 and 32 both have input for row 0, but they output different rows! This is because the permutation maps output row to input row.

So for a single MFMA:

For a 2x2 array of MFMA tiles:

The MFMA instruction has specific input and output layouts:
- Input: lane l holds data for row permute_row(l % 32)
- Output: lane l produces data for rows 16*(l//32) + t

When we compute C = A @ B:

So if lane l produces output row r, it should have A data for row r. But the MFMA input layout says lane l has A data for row permute_row(l % 32).

This means:

- But MFMA says lane 0 has A data for row permute_row(0) = 0

So lane 0 has A data for row 0, and produces output for rows 0-15. This works because all lanes contribute to all outputs through the MFMA reduction.

The MFMA internally handles the data distribution. The key is that:
- We need to provide A data in the layout that MFMA expects
- We get output in the layout that MFMA produces

For the 2x2 tile case:
- We want to compute C[0:64, 0:64]
- Tile (0,0): C[0:32, 0:32]
- Tile (0,1): C[0:32, 32:64]

But the issue is that for acc00 and acc01, we're computing C[0:32, :] using A[0:32, :]. But MFMA expects A data for rows permute_row(l % 32).

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test with 2x2 MFMA tiles (like the full kernel)
# 64x64 output per warp, split into 2x2 array of 32x32 MFMA tiles

M_FLAT = 64
OUT_CHANNELS = 64
K_FLAT = 16
KERNEL_AREA = 4  # 2x2 kernel for simplicity

WARP_SIZE = 64
NUM_WARPS = 1

@substrate.jit
def gemm_2x2_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 16), S.f32),  # Input A
    B: S.Tensor((64, 16), S.f32),  # Weights B (transposed for simplicity)
    C: S.Tensor((64, 64), S.f32),  # Output C
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # Accumulators for 2x2 MFMA tiles
    acc00 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc01 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc10 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc11 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # K tiles: K_FLAT = 16, MFMA_TILE_K = 8, so 2 tiles
    for k_tile in S.range(2):
        a_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        b_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
        b_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

        for e in S.range(4):
            a_frag0[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            a_frag1[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            b_frag0[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)
            b_frag1[e] = S.convert(0.0, S.bf16)

        # For A: first 32 rows and next 32 rows
        m0 = lane_col  # Row 0-31
        m1 = lane_col + 32  # Row 32-63

        # For B: first 32 columns and next 32 columns
        n0 = lane_col  # Column 0-31
        n1 = lane_col + 32  # Column 32-63

        for e in S.range(4):
            k_idx = k_tile * 8 + lane_k_base + e

            # Load A fragments
            if m0 < M_FLAT:
                a_frag0[e] = S.convert(A[m0, k_idx], S.bf16)
            if m1 < M_FLAT:
                a_frag1[e] = S.convert(A[m1, k_idx], S.bf16)

            # Load B fragments
            if n0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
                b_frag0[e] = S.convert(B[n0, k_idx], S.bf16)
            if n1 < OUT_CHANNELS:
                b_frag1[e] = S.convert(B[n1, k_idx], S.bf16)

        # MFMA operations
        acc00 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag0, acc00)
        acc01 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag1, acc01)
        acc10 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag0, acc10)
        acc11 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag1, acc11)

    # Writeback
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row0 = 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx  # Row within first 32 rows
        row1 = 32 + 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx  # Row within second 32 rows
        col0 = lane_col  # Column within first 32 columns
        col1 = lane_col + 32  # Column within second 32 columns

        if row0 < M_FLAT and col0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            C[row0, col0] = acc00[acc_idx]
        if row0 < M_FLAT and col1 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            C[row0, col1] = acc01[acc_idx]
        if row1 < M_FLAT and col0 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            C[row1, col0] = acc10[acc_idx]
        if row1 < M_FLAT and col1 < OUT_CHANNELS:
            C[row1, col1] = acc11[acc_idx]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    A = torch.randn((64, 16), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    B = torch.randn((64, 16), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    C = torch.zeros((64, 64), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    # Expected: C = A @ B.T
    expected = A @ B.T

    gemm_2x2_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (64, 1, 1))](A, B, C)

    actual = C.cpu()
    expected_cpu = expected.cpu()

    max_diff = torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected_cpu)).item()
    print(f'Max diff: {max_diff}')
    print(f'Pass: {torch.allclose(actual, expected_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')

    if max_diff > 0.1:
        print(f'\nFirst row comparison:')
        print(f'actual[0]: {actual[0, :8]}')
        print(f'expected[0]: {expected_cpu[0, :8]}')
        print(f'\nRow 32 comparison:')
        print(f'actual[32]: {actual[32, :8]}')
        print(f'expected[32]: {expected_cpu[32, :8]}')

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test with 2x2 MFMA tiles - debug version
# Focus on understanding the MFMA behavior

M = 64
N = 64
K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64

def permute_row(row: int) -> int:
    high = (row >> 2) & 0x7
    rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
    return (row & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

@substrate.jit
def gemm_2x2_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 64), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # Accumulators for 2x2 MFMA tiles
    acc00 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc01 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc10 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc11 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # Apply permutation for MFMA input layout
    high = (lane_col >> 2) & 0x7
    rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
    permuted_row = (lane_col & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

    a_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test with 2x2 MFMA tiles - debug version
# Focus on understanding the MFMA behavior

M = 64
N = 64
K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64

def permute_row(row: int) -> int:
    high = (row >> 2) & 0x7
    rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
    return (row & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

@substrate.jit
def gemm_2x2_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 64), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # Accumulators for 2x2 MFMA tiles
    acc00 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc01 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc10 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc11 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    high = (lane_col >> 2) & 0x7
    rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
    permuted_row = (lane_col & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

    a_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

    acc01 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag1, acc01)

    # Writeback
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row0 = 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx
        row1 = 32 + 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx
        col0 = lane_col
        col1 = lane_col + 32

        C[row0, col0] = acc00[acc_idx]
        C[row0, col1] = acc01[acc_idx]
        C[row1, col0] = acc10[acc_idx]
        C[row1, col1] = acc11[acc_idx]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Create test matrices
    A = torch.randn((M, K), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    B = torch.randn((N, K), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test single 32x32 MFMA first

M = 32
N = 32
K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64

def permute_row(row: int) -> int:
    high = (row >> 2) & 0x7
    rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
    return (row & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

@substrate.jit
def gemm_single_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((32, 8), S.f32),
    B: S.Tensor((32, 8), S.f32),
    C: S.Tensor((32, 32), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    acc = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # Apply permutation for MFMA input layout
    high = (lane_col >> 2) & 0x7
    rotated = ((high & 0x1) << 2) | (high >> 1)
    permuted_row = (lane_col & 0x3) | (rotated << 2)

    a_frag = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        a_frag[e] = S.convert(A[permuted_row, k_idx], S.bf16)
        b_frag[e] = S.convert(B[lane_col, k_idx], S.bf16)

    acc = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag, b_frag, acc)

    # Writeback
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row = 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx
        col = lane_col
        C[row, col] = acc[acc_idx]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    A = torch.randn((M, K), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    B = torch.randn((N, K), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    C = torch.zeros((M, N), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    expected = A @ B.T

    gemm_single_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (64, 1, 1))](A, B, C)

    actual = C.cpu()
    expected_cpu = expected.cpu()

    max_diff = torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected_cpu)).item()
    print(f'Single tile test:')
    print(f'  Max diff: {max_diff}')
    print(f'  Pass: {torch.allclose(actual, expected_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test 2x2 MFMA tiles WITHOUT permutation

M = 64
N = 64
K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64

@substrate.jit
def gemm_2x2_no_perm_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 64), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # Accumulators for 2x2 MFMA tiles
    acc00 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc01 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc10 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc11 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    a_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e

        # NO permutation
        m0 = lane_col
        m1 = lane_col + 32

def gemm_2x2_no_perm_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 64), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

The 2x2 tile test still fails, and rows 0 and 32 are still equal. This confirms the issue is NOT with permutation, but with the 2x2 tile logic.

Let me think about this more carefully. The single tile works:
- a_frag contains A[lane_col, :] for all lanes
- b_frag contains B[lane_col, :] for all lanes
- MFMA computes C = A @ B.T

For the 2x2 tiles, I'm trying to compute:
- acc00: A[0:32, :] @ B[0:32, :].T
- acc01: A[0:32, :] @ B[32:64, :].T
- acc10: A[32:64, :] @ B[0:32, :].T
- acc11: A[32:64, :] @ B[32:64, :].T

But the MFMA instruction expects data in a specific layout. Each lane provides:
- 4 bf16 values for A (from a specific row)
- 4 bf16 values for B (from a specific column)

The MFMA then computes the 32x32 matrix product where:
- Lane l outputs values for rows 16*(l//32)+t, column l%32

For a single MFMA:
- Lanes 0-31 have A data for rows 0-31
- Lanes 32-63 have A data for rows 0-31 (same, different K values)
- Lanes 0-31 have B data for columns 0-31
- Lanes 32-63 have B data for columns 0-31 (same, different K values)

Wait, that's not right. Let me trace through more carefully.

For the single tile test:
- lane_col = lane % 32
- lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4
- permuted_row = permute_row(lane_col)

For lane 0: lane_col=0, lane_k_base=0, permuted_row=0
For lane 32: lane_col=0, lane_k_base=4, permuted_row=0

Both load from the same row (permuted_row=0), but different K values (0-3 vs 4-7).

So for the A matrix:
- Lanes 0-31: rows permute_row(0-31), K values 0-3
- Lanes 32-63: rows permute_row(0-31), K values 4-7

This means ALL 64 lanes load from the same 32 rows of A, just different K values.

And for the B matrix:
- Lanes 0-31: columns 0-31, K values 0-3
- Lanes 32-63: columns 0-31, K values 4-7

Same columns, different K values.

Now, for the output:
- Lanes 0-31: rows 0-15, columns 0-31
- Lanes 32-63: rows 16-31, columns 0-31

This works because the MFMA accumulates the K values across all lanes.

Now, for the 2x2 tile case:
- a_frag0: rows permute_row(0-31), K values 0-7 (from lanes 0-63)
- a_frag1: rows permute_row(0-31) + 32, K values 0-7 (from lanes 0-63)

Wait, this is the issue! For a_frag1, I'm loading from rows 32-63, but using the same lane_col mapping. This means:
- a_frag0 has rows 0-31 (permuted)
- a_frag1 has rows 32-63 (permuted, shifted by 32)

But when I run MFMA on a_frag0 and a_frag1, the MFMA instruction computes the SAME 32x32 matrix product (because the MFMA always outputs to rows 0-31 in its coordinate system).

The MFMA instruction:
- Takes A fragment and B fragment
- Computes C = A @ B.T + C
- Outputs to 32x32 matrix

So both acc00 and acc10 compute 32x32 matrices, but:
- acc00 uses a_frag0 (A rows 0-31)
- acc10 uses a_frag1 (A rows 32-63)

The OUTPUT should be different because the A data is different. But the MFMA output layout is the same - both output to "rows 0-31" in the MFMA coordinate system.

So the issue is NOT with the MFMA output. The issue is that the same MFMA output (from different MFMA operations) is being written to different rows in C.

But wait, I'm writing:
- acc00 to rows 0-31
- acc10 to rows 32-63

And acc00 and acc10 have the same values. This means the MFMA operations are producing the same output.

Let me check if a_frag0 and a_frag1 actually have different data. The issue might be that a_frag1 is being loaded incorrectly.

Actually, looking at the code:
```python
m0 = lane_col
m1 = lane_col + 32

a_frag0[e] = S.convert(A[m0, k_idx], S.bf16)
a_frag1[e] = S.convert(A[m1, k_idx], S.bf16)
```

For lane 0: m0=0, m1=32
- a_frag0 = A[0, :]
- a_frag1 = A[32, :]

These are different rows. But when I run MFMA with a_frag0 and a_frag1, I get the same output.

Wait, the MFMA instruction accumulates into the output. Let me check if acc10 is being accumulated incorrectly.

Actually, I think I see the issue. The MFMA instruction is:
- acc = MFMA(a_frag, b_frag, acc)

The third argument is the initial accumulator value. For:
- acc00 = MFMA(a_frag0, b_frag0, acc00) - acc00 starts at 0
- acc10 = MFMA(a_frag1, b_frag0, acc10) - acc10 starts at 0

These should be independent. But maybe the compiler is reusing registers incorrectly?

Let me try a different approach: compute the tiles sequentially instead of in parallel.

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test 2x2 MFMA tiles computed SEQUENTIALLY

M = 64
N = 64
K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64

@substrate.jit
def gemm_2x2_sequential_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    B: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    C: S.Tensor((64, 64), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # Initialize all accumulators
    acc00 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc01 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc10 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    acc11 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # Load A fragment for first tile row (rows 0-31)
    a_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        a_frag0[e] = S.convert(A[lane_col, k_idx], S.bf16)

    # Load B fragment for first tile column (columns 0-31)
    b_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        b_frag0[e] = S.convert(B[lane_col, k_idx], S.bf16)

    # Compute tile (0,0)
    acc00 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag0, acc00)

    # Load B fragment for second tile column (columns 32-63)
    b_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        b_frag1[e] = S.convert(B[lane_col + 32, k_idx], S.bf16)

    # Compute tile (0,1)
    acc01 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag1, acc01)

    # Load A fragment for second tile row (rows 32-63)
    a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        a_frag1[e] = S.convert(A[lane_col + 32, k_idx], S.bf16)

    # Compute tile (1,0) - need to reload b_frag0
    b_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        b_frag0[e] = S.convert(B[lane_col, k_idx], S.bf16)

    acc10 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag0, acc10)

    # Reload B fragment for second tile column
    b_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        b_frag1[e] = S.convert(B[lane_col + 32, k_idx], S.bf16)

    # Compute tile (1,1)
    acc11 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag1, acc11)

    # Writeback
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row0 = 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx
        row1 = 32 + 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx
        col0 = lane_col
        col1 = lane_col + 32

        C[row0, col0] = acc00[acc_idx]
        C[row0, col1] = acc01[acc_idx]
        C[row1, col0] = acc10[acc_idx]
        C[row1, col1] = acc11[acc_idx]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    A = torch.randn((M, K), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    B = torch.randn((N, K), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    C = torch.zeros((M, N), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    expected = A @ B.T

    gemm_2x2_sequential_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (64, 1, 1))](A, B, C)

    actual = C.cpu()
    expected_cpu = expected.cpu()

    max_diff = torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected_cpu)).item()
    print(f'2x2 tile sequential:')
    print(f'  Max diff: {max_diff}')
    print(f'  Pass: {torch.allclose(actual, expected_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')

    print(f'\nRow 0 vs Row 32:')
    print(f'  Are they equal? {torch.allclose(actual[0], actual[32])}')

    # Check each tile
    print(f'\nTile (0,0): {torch.max(torch.abs(actual[:32, :32] - expected_cpu[:32, :32])).item():.4f}')
    print(f'Tile (0,1): {torch.max(torch.abs(actual[:32, 32:] - expected_cpu[:32, 32:])).item():.4f}')
    print(f'Tile (1,0): {torch.max(torch.abs(actual[32:, :32] - expected_cpu[32:, :32])).item():.4f}')
    print(f'Tile (1,1): {torch.max(torch.abs(actual[32:, 32:] - expected_cpu[32:, 32:])).item():.4f}')

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test to verify loading from different rows

M = 64
K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64

@substrate.jit
def load_test_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    out: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),  # Output to verify loading
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # Load from row lane_col
    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        out[lane, k_idx] = A[lane_col, e]

    # Also write lane_col to verify
    out[lane, 4] = lane_col


if __name__ == "__main__":

    out = torch.zeros((64, 8), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    load_test_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (64, 1, 1))](A, out)

    out_cpu = out.cpu()
    A_cpu = A.cpu()

    print("Verifying loading pattern:")
    print(f"lane 0: lane_col = {out_cpu[0, 4].item()}, loaded A[{out_cpu[0, 4].item()}, :4] = {out_cpu[0, :4]}")
    print(f"         A[0, :4] = {A_cpu[0, :4]}")

    print(f"lane 31: lane_col = {out_cpu[31, 4].item()}, loaded A[{out_cpu[31, 4].item()}, :4] = {out_cpu[31, :4]}")
    print(f"         A[31, :4] = {A_cpu[31, :4]}")

    print(f"lane 32: lane_col = {out_cpu[32, 4].item()}, loaded A[{out_cpu[32, 4].item()}, :4] = {out_cpu[32, :4]}")
    print(f"         A[0, :4] = {A_cpu[0, :4]}")

    print(f"lane 63: lane_col = {out_cpu[63, 4].item()}, loaded A[{out_cpu[63, 4].item()}, :4] = {out_cpu[63, :4]}")
    print(f"         A[31, :4] = {A_cpu[31, :4]}")


# Now test loading from shifted rows
@substrate.jit
def load_shifted_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    out: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # Load from row lane_col + 32
    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        out[lane, k_idx] = A[lane_col + 32, e]

    A: S.Tensor((64, 8), S.f32),
    out: S.Tensor((64, 4), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32

    # Load from row lane_col + 32, write to out[lane, :]
    for e in S.range(4):
        out[lane, e] = A[lane_col + 32, e]


if __name__ == "__main__":

    out = torch.zeros((64, 4), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    A_cpu = A.cpu()

        lane_col = lane % 32
        print(f"  lane {lane}: lane_col={lane_col}, out={out_cpu[lane].tolist()}")
        print(f"           A[{lane_col}, :4] = {A_cpu[lane_col, :4].tolist()}")
        match = torch.allclose(out_cpu[lane], A_cpu[lane_col, :4])
        print(f"           Match: {match}")

    print("\n\nVerifying shifted loading pattern (from row lane_col + 32):")
    out2 = torch.zeros((64, 4), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    load_shifted_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (64, 1, 1))](A, out2)

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test two MFMA operations with different A data

M = 32
N = 32
K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64

@substrate.jit
def two_mfma_kernel(
    A0: S.Tensor((32, 8), S.f32),
    A1: S.Tensor((32, 8), S.f32),
    B: S.Tensor((32, 8), S.f32),
    C0: S.Tensor((32, 32), S.f32),
    C1: S.Tensor((32, 32), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # First MFMA with A0
    acc0 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    a_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        a_frag0[e] = S.convert(A0[lane_col, k_idx], S.bf16)
        b_frag[e] = S.convert(B[lane_col, k_idx], S.bf16)

    acc0 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag, acc0)

    # Second MFMA with A1
    acc1 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        a_frag1[e] = S.convert(A1[lane_col, k_idx], S.bf16)

    acc1 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag, acc1)

    # Writeback
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row = 16 * (lane // 32) + acc_idx
        col = lane_col
        C0[row, col] = acc0[acc_idx]
        C1[row, col] = acc1[acc_idx]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    A0 = torch.randn((32, 8), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    A1 = torch.randn((32, 8), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    B = torch.randn((32, 8), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    C0 = torch.zeros((32, 32), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    C1 = torch.zeros((32, 32), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    expected0 = A0 @ B.T
    expected1 = A1 @ B.T

    two_mfma_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (64, 1, 1))](A0, A1, B, C0, C1)

    actual0 = C0.cpu()
    actual1 = C1.cpu()
    expected0_cpu = expected0.cpu()
    expected1_cpu = expected1.cpu()

    diff0 = torch.max(torch.abs(actual0 - expected0_cpu)).item()
    diff1 = torch.max(torch.abs(actual1 - expected1_cpu)).item()

    print(f'Two MFMA test:')
    print(f'  C0 (A0 @ B.T): max_diff={diff0:.4f}, pass={torch.allclose(actual0, expected0_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')
    print(f'  C1 (A1 @ B.T): max_diff={diff1:.4f}, pass={torch.allclose(actual1, expected1_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')

    # Check if C0 and C1 are different
    print(f'\n  C0 == C1? {torch.allclose(actual0, actual1)}')
    print(f'  C0[0, :4] = {actual0[0, :4].tolist()}')
    print(f'  C1[0, :4] = {actual1[0, :4].tolist()}')

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Simple 32x32x8 GEMM test - NO PERMUTATION
M = 32
N = 32
K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64

@substrate.jit
def gemm_kernel(
    A: S.Tensor((32, 8), S.bf16),
    B: S.Tensor((8, 32), S.bf16),
    C: S.Tensor((32, 32), S.f32),
):
    lane = S.thread_id(0)
    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    acc = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)

    # NO permutation - direct row mapping
    a_row = lane_col
    b_col = lane_col

    # Load 4 bf16 values into local buffers
    a_frag_local = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag_local = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

    for e in S.range(4):
        k = lane_k_base + e
        a_frag_local[e] = A[a_row, k]
        b_frag_local[e] = B[k, b_col]

    acc = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag_local, b_frag_local, acc)

    # Writeback - standard MFMA output layout
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row = 8 * (acc_idx // 4) + 4 * (lane // 32) + (acc_idx % 4)
        col = lane_col
        C[row, col] = acc[acc_idx]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Test
    A = torch.randn((32, 8), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    B = torch.randn((8, 32), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    C = torch.zeros((32, 32), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    expected = (A.float() @ B.float())

    gemm_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (WARP_SIZE, 1, 1))](A, B, C)

    actual = C.cpu()
    expected_cpu = expected.cpu()

    max_diff = torch.max(torch.abs(actual - expected_cpu)).item()
    print(f'Max diff: {max_diff}')
    print(f'Pass: {torch.allclose(actual, expected_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')

    # Check which rows match
    for r in range(0, 32, 4):
        row_diff = torch.max(torch.abs(actual[r] - expected_cpu[r])).item()
        print(f'Row {r} diff: {row_diff}')

content.thinking:

import torch
import substrate
import substrate.language as S

# Test two MFMA operations with bf16 inputs (no convert)

M = 32
N = 32
K = 8
WARP_SIZE = 64

@substrate.jit
def two_mfma_kernel(
    A0: S.Tensor((32, 8), S.bf16),
    A1: S.Tensor((32, 8), S.bf16),
    B: S.Tensor((32, 8), S.bf16),
    C0: S.Tensor((32, 32), S.f32),
    C1: S.Tensor((32, 32), S.f32),
):
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    lane = tid % 64

    lane_col = lane % 32
    lane_k_base = (lane // 32) * 4

    # First MFMA with A0
    acc0 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    a_frag0 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)
    b_frag = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        a_frag0[e] = A0[lane_col, k_idx]
        b_frag[e] = B[lane_col, k_idx]

    acc0 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag0, b_frag, acc0)

    # Second MFMA with A1
    acc1 = S.full((16,), 0.0, S.f32)
    a_frag1 = S.make_local((4,), S.bf16)

    for e in S.range(4):
        k_idx = lane_k_base + e
        a_frag1[e] = A1[lane_col, k_idx]

    acc1 = S.amdgpu.mfma_32x32x8_bf16_f32(a_frag1, b_frag, acc1)

    # Writeback using the working formula from test_gemm3.py
    for acc_idx in S.range(16):
        row = 8 * (acc_idx // 4) + 4 * (lane // 32) + (acc_idx % 4)
        col = lane_col
        C0[row, col] = acc0[acc_idx]
        C1[row, col] = acc1[acc_idx]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    A0 = torch.randn((32, 8), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    A1 = torch.randn((32, 8), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    B = torch.randn((32, 8), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device='cuda')
    C0 = torch.zeros((32, 32), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')
    C1 = torch.zeros((32, 32), dtype=torch.float32, device='cuda')

    expected0 = A0.float() @ B.float().T
    expected1 = A1.float() @ B.float().T

    two_mfma_kernel[lambda: ((1, 1, 1), (64, 1, 1))](A0, A1, B, C0, C1)

    actual0 = C0.cpu()
    actual1 = C1.cpu()
    expected0_cpu = expected0.cpu()
    expected1_cpu = expected1.cpu()

    diff0 = torch.max(torch.abs(actual0 - expected0_cpu)).item()
    diff1 = torch.max(torch.abs(actual1 - expected1_cpu)).item()

    print(f'Two MFMA test with bf16 inputs:')
    print(f'  C0 (A0 @ B.T): max_diff={diff0:.6f}, pass={torch.allclose(actual0, expected0_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')
    print(f'  C1 (A1 @ B.T): max_diff={diff1:.6f}, pass={torch.allclose(actual1, expected1_cpu, rtol=1e-2, atol=0.1)}')

    # Check if C0 and C1 are different
    print(f'\n  C0 == C1? {torch.allclose(actual0, actual1)}')
    print(f'  C0[0, :4] = {actual0[0, :4].tolist()}')
    print(f'  C1[0, :4] = {actual1[0, :4].tolist()}')

"""AMDGPU BF16 flash attention through masked softmax."""

import math
import substrate
import substrate.language as S
import torch

WARP_SIZE = 64
NUM_WARPS = 8
THREADS = WARP_SIZE * NUM_WARPS

BLOCK_ROWS = 256
BLOCK_COLS = 64
BC_SLICES = 2
BC_PER_SLICE = BLOCK_COLS // BC_SLICES
Q_WARP_GROUPS = 2
Q_WARPS_PER_GROUP = NUM_WARPS // Q_WARP_GROUPS
Q_STAGE_ROWS = BLOCK_ROWS // Q_WARP_GROUPS
HEAD_DIM = 128
VEC_SIZE = 16 // 2
BF16_BYTES = 2
U128_BYTES = VEC_SIZE * BF16_BYTES

MMA_N = 32
MMA_K = 8
SHM_TILE_M = 32
SHM_TILE_N = 16
QK_COL_TILES = BLOCK_COLS // SHM_TILE_M
O_TILES = HEAD_DIM // MMA_N
Q_BATCH_TILES = HEAD_DIM // SHM_TILE_N
O_CHUNK_PER_SLICE = BC_PER_SLICE // SHM_TILE_N
MFMA_ACC_SIZE = 16
QK_MMAS_PER_SLICE = Q_BATCH_TILES * 2
QK_MMAS_MAJOR = (QK_MMAS_PER_SLICE * 3) // 4
QK_MMAS_MINOR = QK_MMAS_PER_SLICE - QK_MMAS_MAJOR

SHM_Q_SIZE = BLOCK_ROWS * HEAD_DIM
SHM_KV_SIZE = BLOCK_COLS * HEAD_DIM
SHM_Q_STAGE_WORDS = Q_STAGE_ROWS * HEAD_DIM // 2
SHM_K_SLICE_WORDS = BC_PER_SLICE * HEAD_DIM // 2
SHM_K_WORDS = BLOCK_COLS * HEAD_DIM // 2
SHM_Q_VECS = BLOCK_ROWS * HEAD_DIM // VEC_SIZE
SHM_Q_STAGE_VECS = Q_STAGE_ROWS * HEAD_DIM // VEC_SIZE
SHM_K_SLICE_VECS = BC_PER_SLICE * HEAD_DIM // VEC_SIZE
SHM_K_VECS = BLOCK_COLS * HEAD_DIM // VEC_SIZE
SHM_V_SLICE_VECS = BC_PER_SLICE * HEAD_DIM // VEC_SIZE
SHM_K_TOTAL_WORDS = BC_SLICES * SHM_K_SLICE_WORDS
SHM_K_TOTAL_VECS = BC_SLICES * SHM_K_SLICE_VECS
SHM_DWORDS = SHM_Q_STAGE_WORDS + SHM_K_WORDS
SHM_O_DWORDS = BLOCK_ROWS * HEAD_DIM // 2
SHM_SHARED_DWORDS = max(SHM_DWORDS, SHM_O_DWORDS)

NEG_INF = -1.0e30
SCALE_LOG2 = math.log2(math.e) / math.sqrt(HEAD_DIM)
BF16_PACK_SELECTOR = 0x07060302

def _validate_packed_flash_attn_inputs(
    q: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
    v: torch.Tensor,
    seq_ptr: torch.Tensor,
):
    if q.ndim != 3 or k.ndim != 3 or v.ndim != 3:
        raise ValueError(
            "q, k, and v must be rank-3 packed tensors shaped [tokens, heads, dim] "
            f"(got q.ndim={q.ndim}, k.ndim={k.ndim}, v.ndim={v.ndim})"
        )
    if q.shape[-1] != HEAD_DIM or k.shape[-1] != HEAD_DIM or v.shape[-1] != HEAD_DIM:
        raise ValueError(
            f"BF16 flash attention currently requires head_dim={HEAD_DIM} "
            f"(got q={q.shape[-1]}, k={k.shape[-1]}, v={v.shape[-1]})"
        )
    if q.shape[0] != k.shape[0] or q.shape[0] != v.shape[0]:
        raise ValueError(
            f"q, k, and v must have the same token dimension (got {q.shape[0]}, {k.shape[0]}, {v.shape[0]})"
        )
    if k.shape[1] != v.shape[1]:
        raise ValueError(f"k and v must have the same number of KV heads (got {k.shape[1]}, {v.shape[1]})")
    if q.shape[1] == 0 or k.shape[1] == 0:
        raise ValueError("q_heads and kv_heads must be nonzero")
    if q.shape[1] % k.shape[1] != 0:
        raise ValueError(f"q_heads must be divisible by kv_heads (got {q.shape[1]} and {k.shape[1]})")
    if q.dtype != torch.bfloat16 or k.dtype != torch.bfloat16 or v.dtype != torch.bfloat16:
        raise TypeError(
            f"q, k, and v must be torch.bfloat16 (got q={q.dtype}, k={k.dtype}, v={v.dtype})"
        )
    if q.device.type != "cuda" or k.device.type != "cuda" or v.device.type != "cuda":
        raise ValueError(
            f"q, k, and v must be CUDA tensors (got q={q.device}, k={k.device}, v={v.device})"
        )
    if q.device != k.device or q.device != v.device:
        raise ValueError(
            f"q, k, and v must be on the same device (got {q.device}, {k.device}, {v.device})"
        )
    if not q.is_contiguous() or not k.is_contiguous() or not v.is_contiguous():
        raise ValueError("q, k, and v must be contiguous")

    if not isinstance(seq_ptr, torch.Tensor):
        raise TypeError("seq_ptr must be torch.Tensor")
    if seq_ptr.ndim != 1:

867	def _flash_attn_kernel(
868	     out_ptr: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
869	     q_ptr: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
870	     k_ptr: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
871	     v_ptr: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
872	     seq_ptr: S.Pointer(S.u32),
873	     gqa_ratio: S.u32,
874	     heads_kv: S.u32,
875	):
876	    heads_q = heads_kv * gqa_ratio
877	    stride_q = heads_q * HEAD_DIM
878	    stride_kv = heads_kv * HEAD_DIM
879	
880	    g_seq = S.make_tensor(seq_ptr, S.u32, S.make_layout((S.grid_dim(2) + 1,), (1,)))
881	
882	    idx_q = S.block_id(0)
883	    idx_h = S.block_id(1)
884	    seq_id = S.block_id(2)
885	    tid = S.thread_id(0)
886	    seq_begin = g_seq[seq_id]
887	    seq_end = g_seq[seq_id + 1]
888	    seq_len = seq_end - seq_begin 
889	    wid = tid // WARP_SIZE
890	    wtid = tid % WARP_SIZE
891	
892	    q_memref = S.make_tensor(q_ptr, S.bf16, S.make_layout((seq_end * stride_q,), (1,)))
893	    k_memref = S.make_tensor(k_ptr, S.bf16, S.make_layout((seq_end * stride_kv,), (1,)))
894	    v_memref = S.make_tensor(v_ptr, S.bf16, S.make_layout((seq_end * stride_kv,), (1,)))
895	    out_vec_memref = S.make_tensor(out_ptr, S.u32, S.make_layout((seq_end * stride_q // VEC_SIZE, 4), (4, 1)))
896	    q_rsrc = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(q_memref, seq_end * stride_q * BF16_BYTES)
897	    k_rsrc = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(k_memref, seq_end * stride_kv * BF16_BYTES)
898	    v_rsrc = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(v_memref, seq_end * stride_kv * BF16_BYTES)
899	    l_q_off = (seq_begin * stride_q + idx_q * BLOCK_ROWS * stride_q + idx_h * HEAD_DIM) * BF16_BYTES 
900	    l_k_off = (seq_begin * stride_kv + (idx_h // gqa_ratio) * HEAD_DIM) * BF16_BYTES
901	    l_v_off = (seq_begin * stride_kv + (idx_h // gqa_ratio) * HEAD_DIM) * BF16_BYTES
902	
903	    shm_buf = S.make_shared((SHM_SHARED_DWORDS // 4, 4), S.u32)
904	    shm_o = S.view(shm_buf, S.Tensor((BLOCK_ROWS * HEAD_DIM // 4,), S.u64))
905	    s_k0_u128 = S.subview(shm_buf, (0, 0), (SHM_K_SLICE_VECS, 4), (1, 1))
906	    s_k1_u128 = S.subview(shm_buf, (SHM_K_SLICE_VECS, 0), (SHM_K_SLICE_VECS, 4), (1, 1))
907	    s_q_u128 = S.subview(shm_buf, (SHM_K_TOTAL_VECS, 0), (SHM_Q_STAGE_VECS, 4), (1, 1))
908	    s_v_u128 = S.subview(shm_buf, (SHM_K_TOTAL_VECS, 0), (SHM_V_SLICE_VECS, 4), (1, 1))
909	
910	    if idx_q * BLOCK_ROWS >= seq_len:
911	        return

def _flash_attn_kernel(
     out_ptr: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
     q_ptr: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
     k_ptr: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
     v_ptr: S.Pointer(S.bf16),
     seq_ptr: S.Pointer(S.u32),
     gqa_ratio: S.u32,
     heads_kv: S.u32,
):
    heads_q = heads_kv * gqa_ratio
    stride_q = heads_q * HEAD_DIM
    stride_kv = heads_kv * HEAD_DIM

    g_seq = S.make_tensor(seq_ptr, S.u32, S.make_layout((S.grid_dim(2) + 1,), (1,)))

    idx_q = S.block_id(0)
    idx_h = S.block_id(1)
    seq_id = S.block_id(2)
    tid = S.thread_id(0)
    seq_begin = g_seq[seq_id]
    seq_end = g_seq[seq_id + 1]
    seq_len = seq_end - seq_begin 
    wid = tid // WARP_SIZE
    wtid = tid % WARP_SIZE

    q_memref = S.make_tensor(q_ptr, S.bf16, S.make_layout((seq_end * stride_q,), (1,)))
    k_memref = S.make_tensor(k_ptr, S.bf16, S.make_layout((seq_end * stride_kv,), (1,)))
    v_memref = S.make_tensor(v_ptr, S.bf16, S.make_layout((seq_end * stride_kv,), (1,)))
    out_vec_memref = S.make_tensor(out_ptr, S.u32, S.make_layout((seq_end * stride_q // VEC_SIZE, 4), (4, 1)))
    q_rsrc = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(q_memref, seq_end * stride_q * BF16_BYTES)
    k_rsrc = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(k_memref, seq_end * stride_kv * BF16_BYTES)
    v_rsrc = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(v_memref, seq_end * stride_kv * BF16_BYTES)
    l_q_off = (seq_begin * stride_q + idx_q * BLOCK_ROWS * stride_q + idx_h * HEAD_DIM) * BF16_BYTES 
    l_k_off = (seq_begin * stride_kv + (idx_h // gqa_ratio) * HEAD_DIM) * BF16_BYTES
    l_v_off = (seq_begin * stride_kv + (idx_h // gqa_ratio) * HEAD_DIM) * BF16_BYTES

    shm_buf = S.make_shared((SHM_SHARED_DWORDS // 4, 4), S.u32)
    shm_o = S.view(shm_buf, S.Tensor((BLOCK_ROWS * HEAD_DIM // 4,), S.u64))
    s_k0_u128 = S.subview(shm_buf, (0, 0), (SHM_K_SLICE_VECS, 4), (1, 1))
    s_k1_u128 = S.subview(shm_buf, (SHM_K_SLICE_VECS, 0), (SHM_K_SLICE_VECS, 4), (1, 1))
    s_q_u128 = S.subview(shm_buf, (SHM_K_TOTAL_VECS, 0), (SHM_Q_STAGE_VECS, 4), (1, 1))
    s_v_u128 = S.subview(shm_buf, (SHM_K_TOTAL_VECS, 0), (SHM_V_SLICE_VECS, 4), (1, 1))

    if idx_q * BLOCK_ROWS >= seq_len:
        return

    q_lds_base_bytes = SHM_K_TOTAL_WORDS * 4
    k1_lds_base_bytes = SHM_K_SLICE_WORDS * 4
    group_wid = wid % Q_WARPS_PER_GROUP
    wgid = wid // Q_WARPS_PER_GROUP
    r_q = S.make_local((Q_BATCH_TILES, 2, 4), S.bf16)
    _load_q_2stage(r_q, shm_buf, s_q_u128, stride_q, q_rsrc, l_q_off, q_lds_base_bytes, wid, wtid, tid)

    r_o = S.make_local((O_TILES, MFMA_ACC_SIZE), S.f32)
    for i in S.range(O_TILES):
        for j in S.range(MFMA_ACC_SIZE):
            r_o[i, j] = 0

    r_k = S.make_local((Q_BATCH_TILES, 2, 4), S.bf16)
    r_s0 = S.make_local((MFMA_ACC_SIZE,), S.f32)
    r_s0_bf16 = S.make_local((MFMA_ACC_SIZE,), S.bf16)
    mi = S.convert(0.0, S.f32)
    l = S.convert(0.0, S.f32)
    row_idx_base = idx_q * BLOCK_ROWS
    min_k_tile = row_idx_base // BLOCK_COLS
    max_k_col = S.min(seq_len - 1, row_idx_base + BLOCK_ROWS - 1)
    max_k_tile = max_k_col // BLOCK_COLS

    S.amdgpu.s_waitcnt(0, 0, 0)
    S.syncthreads()
    if wgid == 0:
        _tiled_layout_fetch_global_k_to_shm_async_slice(
            shm_buf, stride_kv, k_rsrc, l_k_off, group_wid, wtid, 0
        )
    else:
        _tiled_layout_fetch_global_k_to_shm_async_slice(
            shm_buf,
            stride_kv,
            k_rsrc,
            l_k_off + BC_PER_SLICE * stride_kv * BF16_BYTES,
            group_wid,
            wtid,
            k1_lds_base_bytes,
        )
    S.amdgpu.s_waitcnt(0, 7, 15)
    S.syncthreads()

    _fetch_k_regs_slice(r_k, s_k0_u128, wtid)
    _clear_s_slice(r_s0)
    _gemm_qk_slice(r_s0, r_q, r_k)
    if 0 >= min_k_tile:
        _apply_causal_mask_slice(r_s0, row_idx_base, 0, tid)
    mi = _compute_row_max_slice(r_s0)
    neg_inf = S.convert(NEG_INF, S.f32)
    if mi == neg_inf:
        mi = S.convert(0.0, S.f32)
    _compute_ptilde_slice(r_s0, mi)
    l = _compute_row_sum_slice(r_s0)
    _convert_to_bf16_score_slice(r_s0_bf16, r_s0)
    softmax_state = S.make_local((4,), S.f32)
    softmax_state[0] = mi
    softmax_state[1] = l
    softmax_state[2] = S.convert(0.0, S.f32)
    softmax_state[3] = S.convert(0.0, S.f32)
    if wid < Q_WARPS_PER_GROUP:
        _main_loop_pipelined_exact(
            r_o,
            r_q,
            r_s0_bf16,
            softmax_state,
            shm_buf,
            s_k0_u128,
            s_k1_u128,
            s_v_u128,
            stride_kv,
            k_rsrc,
            v_rsrc,
            l_k_off,
            l_v_off,
            k1_lds_base_bytes,
            row_idx_base,
            min_k_tile,
            max_k_tile,
            wid,
            wtid,
            tid,
            group_wid,
        )
    else:
        _main_loop_pipelined_exact(
            r_o,
            r_q,
            r_s0_bf16,
            softmax_state,
            shm_buf,
            s_k0_u128,
            s_k1_u128,
            s_v_u128,
            stride_kv,
            k_rsrc,
            v_rsrc,
            l_k_off,
            l_v_off,
            k1_lds_base_bytes,
            row_idx_base,
            min_k_tile,
            max_k_tile,
            wid,
            wtid,
            tid,
            group_wid,
        )
    l = softmax_state[1]
    
    r_o_bf16 = S.make_local((O_TILES, MFMA_ACC_SIZE), S.bf16)
    _normalize_softmax_lse(l, r_o_bf16, r_o)

    s_o_vec = S.view(shm_o, S.Tensor((BLOCK_ROWS * HEAD_DIM // VEC_SIZE, 4), S.u32))
    S.syncthreads()
    _unpack_o(shm_o, r_o_bf16, wid, wtid)
    S.syncthreads()

    out_base = (seq_begin * stride_q + idx_q * BLOCK_ROWS * stride_q + idx_h * HEAD_DIM) // VEC_SIZE
    remaining = seq_len - idx_q * BLOCK_ROWS
    valid_rows = remaining
    if BLOCK_ROWS < valid_rows:
        valid_rows = BLOCK_ROWS
    out_range = (out_base + valid_rows * stride_q // VEC_SIZE - stride_q // VEC_SIZE + HEAD_DIM // VEC_SIZE) * U128_BYTES
    out_rsrc = S.amdgpu.make_rsrc(out_vec_memref, out_range)
    _write_result(out_rsrc, s_o_vec, tid, stride_q, out_base)

def _flash_attn_impl(
    q: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
    v: torch.Tensor,
    seq_ptr: torch.Tensor,
    out: torch.Tensor | None = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    _validate_packed_flash_attn_inputs(q, k, v, seq_ptr)

    seq_ptr_host = seq_ptr.to(device="cpu", dtype=torch.int64)
    seq_lens = torch.diff(seq_ptr_host)
    num_seqs = int(seq_ptr_host.numel() - 1)
    max_seq_len = int(seq_lens.max().item())
    num_q_blocks = (max_seq_len + BLOCK_ROWS - 1) // BLOCK_ROWS
    if bool((seq_lens % BLOCK_ROWS).any().item()):
        raise NotImplementedError(
            "flash_attn currently requires every sequence length to be a multiple of BLOCK_ROWS"
        )

    heads_q = q.shape[1]
    heads_kv = k.shape[1]
    gqa_ratio = heads_q // heads_kv

    if out is None:
        out = torch.zeros((q.shape[0], heads_q, HEAD_DIM), dtype=torch.bfloat16, device=q.device)

    seq_ptr_dev = seq_ptr.to(device=q.device, dtype=torch.int32)
    _flash_attn_kernel[lambda: ((num_q_blocks, heads_q, num_seqs), (THREADS, 1, 1))](
        out,
